factorDescription: Measures breakout confirmation by combining 5-day close-location strength with 20-day relative volume and penalizing wide intraday ranges. Higher values indicate a strong close near the high, elevated participation, and compressed daily range, which may support short-horizon continuation.

Captures the interaction between bullish close location and abnormal volume, scaled by recent intraday volatility. This factor is designed to favor days where price closes near the high on strong volume while the recent range remains tight.

Combines a short-term close-strength signal with a relative volume proxy and a smoothing term for intraday range. It aims to identify accumulation-like sessions where the stock closes strongly near the high, volume expands, and the daily range is not excessive.

Measures daily price-volume pressure imbalance by combining the close's location within the intraday range with a 5-day abnormal volume score relative to a 20-day baseline. Strong closes near the high on unusually high volume produce positive values, while weak closes near the low on unusually high volume produce negative values.

Cross-sectionally normalizes the pressure imbalance signal so that the strongest demand- or supply-driven names stand out each day. It uses the same close-location and abnormal-volume mechanism, but applies ranking to improve comparability across instruments.

Adds short-horizon smoothing to the pressure imbalance signal to reduce noise while preserving the core hypothesis. The factor emphasizes persistent demand or supply when the close is consistently near the day extreme under abnormal volume.

Measures short-horizon mean-reversion pressure when the 3-day cumulative return is large relative to its own 5-day volatility and the close is near the recent high-low channel edge. Higher values indicate a more stretched, potentially exhaustion-prone move.

Captures a similar exhaustion-and-reversal effect using a 5-day cumulative move normalized by 5-day volatility, combined with the close's position relative to a 10-day high-low channel. This variant is slower and distinct from the 3-day version.

factorExpression: SIGN(TS_SUM($return, 3)) * ABS(TS_SUM($return, 3)) / (TS_STD($return, 5) + 1e-8) * ABS((2 * $close - TS_MAX($high, 10) - TS_MIN($low, 10)) / (TS_MAX($high, 10) - TS_MIN($low, 10) + 1e-8))
factorDescription: Emphasizes overextension by combining the signed 3-day return shock with the distance of the close from the midpoint of the recent 10-day channel. Values become larger when price has moved strongly and sits near a channel extreme.

Captures short-horizon exhaustion when 10-day cumulative return is strong, the close sits near the top of the recent range, and return volatility is elevated. Higher values indicate greater overextension and a stronger mean-reversion setup over the next few days.

Measures stabilization after trend decay by rewarding stocks whose recent return has cooled, volatility is moderate, and close is nearer the middle of the 20-day range than the extremes. This is designed to favor neutral-to-positive forward returns when the prior move has lost momentum.

Penalizes stocks that combine positive 10-day trend with a close positioned near a recent range extreme and elevated 20-day return volatility. It is a more direct reversal-oriented variant that emphasizes overextension rather than trend continuation.

Captures fresh breakout quality by rewarding closes near the daily high, strong volume relative to a 10-day average, and compressed intraday range. This is intended to proxy accumulation-like continuation setups.

Measures breakout strength while penalizing short-term exhaustion by combining close-to-high position and volume surprise with recent return run-up and volatility. Higher values favor continuation when breakout quality dominates exhaustion.

Rewards a fresh breakout signature only when the stock is not too extended versus its recent high and recent return volatility is contained. This is designed to separate constructive breakouts from late-stage crowded advances.

Regime-aware hybrid signal that rewards close-location buying pressure when recent price stretch is moderate, but penalizes the same pressure when the stock is already extended relative to recent volatility and the 20-day range position. It is designed to capture continuation in healthy demand and reversal after late-stage exhaustion.

A reversal-leaning factor that measures whether strong close-location pressure is occurring near a recent 20-day high or low after a volatility-normalized move has become stretched. It downweights continuation when the price is already near a recent extreme and recent cumulative return is large relative to volatility.

A simpler continuation factor that prefers stocks closing near the top of their daily range on elevated 5-day relative volume, but only when the recent 5-day return is not excessively stretched. This captures demand confirmation while reducing false positives from late-stage moves.

Measures short-term overreaction by combining a standardized one-day return shock with a contrarian sign. Large positive return shocks receive negative scores, while large negative shocks receive positive scores, aligned with next-day mean reversion after extreme moves.

Captures transient dislocation when a wide daily range and abnormal volume accompany an extreme return shock. It is designed to favor reversal when the move is large, the range expands, and volume is elevated relative to recent history.

Measures how far the close is stretched away from its recent center, normalized by recent close volatility. A large deviation suggests short-term overextension and supports a contrarian next-day reversal view.

Measures whether a large opening gap is reinforced by same-day directional confirmation. Higher values favor continuation when the open-to-previous-close gap aligns with an expanded intraday move and close direction.

Captures mean reversion risk after a large gap that is not supported by intraday range expansion. Higher values indicate a large gap with weak same-day confirmation, which is more likely to reverse next day.

Emphasizes whether the opening gap is supported by a strong open-to-close body in the same direction. This is intended to separate accepted breakouts from transient dislocations using a shorter horizon.

Measures whether the opening gap is more predictive when aligned with short-term momentum and elevated contemporaneous volume. Positive recent trend should amplify continuation, while negative trend should dampen or reverse the effect.

Captures a regime-dependent gap effect conditioned on medium-term trend. The factor is designed so that the same opening gap can behave differently in stronger trend regimes versus mean-reverting regimes.

A simpler confirmation-style gap factor that emphasizes opening dislocation only when current volume is unusually high relative to its recent baseline. This is intended to strengthen signals on information-rich days.

Measures whether a stock's recent realized volatility has expanded relative to its own prior compression regime. Higher values indicate a transition from quiet trading to active movement, which may support short-horizon continuation.

Captures the transition from compressed to expanding daily trading range using only high-low range normalized by price. Higher values suggest breakout-prone volatility expansion; lower values suggest range contraction and possible exhaustion.

Measures whether short-term volatility is accelerating relative to a slightly longer baseline. This is designed to isolate regime shifts where volatility is increasing after a period of compression, which may favor continuation over the next day.

Captures a volatility-compression breakout by comparing today’s true range expansion and close location against recent 5-20 day compressed volatility. The factor is positive when range expands after a quiet regime and the close finishes near the high, and negative when the close finishes near the low.

Measures whether today’s range expansion occurs after a compressed range regime. It uses the ratio of the current high-low range to its 20-day average, scaled by the inverse of recent 20-day range volatility so that stronger signals arise after unusually quiet periods.

Emphasizes directional confirmation from close location within the daily range after a short-term breakout-like expansion. The factor is positive when the close is near the high and negative when the close is near the low, with a 10-day smoothing on the close-location signal to reduce noise.

Captures next-day continuation after an opening gap when the stock has been in a recent low-volatility regime, the current day expands its intraday range versus a 10-day baseline, and the close finishes near the day’s high. Positive values favor upside gap continuation; negative values favor downside continuation.

factorExpression: RANK(ABS(($open - DELAY($close, 1)) / (DELAY($close, 1) + 1e-8))) * RANK(1 - ($high - $low) / (TS_MEAN($high - $low, 12) + 1e-8)) * RANK(1 - ABS((($close - $low) - ($high - $close)) / ($high - $low + 1e-8))) * RANK(TS_MAD($high - $low, 12) / (TS_MEAN($high - $low, 12) + 1e-8))
factorDescription: Measures failed-gap mean reversion when the open-to-previous-close gap is large but the day does not confirm with range expansion or close strength. Higher values indicate stronger reversion pressure after unconfirmed gaps.

A simpler regime-aware gap factor that emphasizes gap direction only when recent volatility has been compressed and the current session confirms with an expanded range. This is intended to be more robust and less interaction-heavy than the first factor.

Measures whether the daily open gap is aligned with a positive short-term trend and confirmed by expanding range and elevated volume. Higher values indicate stronger continuation pressure when the gap occurs in a supportive regime.

Captures gap-driven reversal risk when the gap appears after an extended trend and in a weak activity regime. The factor is designed to penalize gaps that occur with stretched recent returns but compressed range and muted volume.

Targets mean reversion after a gap when short-term range expansion fails to confirm the move and volume is not unusually strong. This factor emphasizes gap size relative to recent return variability and discounts it when volatility and participation are weak.

Captures short-medium trend exhaustion by combining 5-day momentum, unusual volume expansion, and weak close location within the daily range. The factor is higher when a recent trend is strong, but the day closes poorly relative to its range while volume is elevated, which is consistent with exhaustion risk.

Measures whether a 10-day trend is being supported by healthy intraday close quality and non-abnormal volume. It favors continuation when momentum is positive, close location is strong, and volume is near normal rather than spiking.

A slower trend-exhaustion signal using 20-day momentum, volume surprise, and a penalty for weak close location. It is designed to detect mature trends that become vulnerable when participation surges but the close fails to hold near the day's extreme.

Measures whether the close finishes near the daily high while volume is above its 20-day average. Positive values indicate strong demand and potential short-horizon continuation; negative values indicate weak closes on light participation or distribution-like behavior.

Combines a close-to-high location score with a standardized volume surprise term. This factor is designed to capture persistent buying pressure when price settles near the upper end of the daily range on unusually active volume.

Measures directional close pressure within the daily range and scales it by the recent change in volume. It emphasizes whether the stock closes near the high on expanding participation, while remaining simple and robust.

Captures short-horizon continuation when recent true-range is compressed, current volume expands versus its 5-day average, and the close finishes near the day high. Higher values indicate stronger bullish breakout confirmation.
Measures bearish continuation when recent range is compressed, volume expands, and the close finishes near the day low. Higher values indicate stronger downside breakdown confirmation.

Combines short-term return direction with compression and volume confirmation over a 5-day lookback. This factor favors positive returns only when they occur after compressed ranges and rising volume, while penalizing moves lacking confirmation.

Measures short-horizon cumulative return relative to recent return volatility. Extreme positive or negative 5-day moves are interpreted as overextension and are expected to mean-revert over the next few days.

Captures how far the current close is from its 10-day moving average. Large deviations from the short-term baseline are treated as exhaustion signals and are expected to predict opposite-direction near-term returns.

Combines volatility-normalized short-term stretch with deviation from the 10-day average close. The factor is designed to strengthen contrarian signals when both recent momentum and baseline displacement indicate overextension.
Scores stocks positively when a recent 10-day range compression is followed by strong close-location and volume expansion versus a 20-day baseline, capturing breakout-style regime transitions.
Scores stocks positively when short-term return stretch is large relative to recent volatility but the close is weak versus a 5-day moving average, capturing exhaustion and mean-reversion pressure.

Combines breakout confirmation and exhaustion reversal into one regime-sensitive score: positive when compression breaks out with volume and close strength, or when a stretched move closes weakly below a short moving average.
Captures short-horizon continuation when the stock closes near the daily high and volume is elevated versus its 20-day baseline. This factor is designed to favor demand-backed strength.

Combines close-location and volume confirmation with penalties for overextension and slowing return momentum. It should be positive when strength is fresh, and reduced when the move is stretched or decelerating.

Measures whether near-high closing and volume expansion are strong enough to overcome recent exhaustion. It uses a 10-day stretch proxy and a 20-day volume surprise against a simple return-deceleration penalty.

Measures short-horizon trend persistence by combining positive cumulative returns, frequent positive daily return signs, and low realized volatility over a 10-day window. Higher values indicate smoother upward drift with less noise.

Captures orderly price drift by rewarding positive close-to-close returns and penalizing unstable daily trading ranges over a 20-day window. This favors names with steady advances and narrow, stable intraday movement.

Measures smooth trend continuation by comparing recent average return against volume-normalized price drift variability over 30 days. Higher values favor persistent upward drift with less noisy volume-adjusted movement.
